item.title: 'Resilience defines economic outlook'
item.description: Bangladesh has demonstrated notable resilience in navigating recent economic headwinds, with growth expected to strengthen gradually over the next few years, according to Frederic Neumann, chief Asia economist and co-head of Global Research Asia at HSBC Global Research. Speaking at an event organi

item.title: Govt cancels DMTCL MD appointment
item.description: The government has cancelled the appointment of Faruque Ahmed as the managing director (MD) of Dhaka Mass Transit Company Limited (DMTCL). A circular, issued on Monday by the Road Transport and Highways Division under the Ministry of Road Transport and Bridges upon the president's order, cancelled

item.title: Advance train ticket sales for Eid-ul-Fitr begin
item.description: Bangladesh Railway has launched advance ticket sales for Eid-ul-Fitr travel, beginning with trains heading towards the western region of the country. The sales began at 8:00 am on Tuesday, specifically for passengers intending to travel on March 13. The entire quota of Eid tickets is being proces

item.title: Crowne Plaza Dhaka Airport, Brac Bank sign MoU
item.description: Crowne Plaza Dhaka Airport, one of the flagships IHG properties in Bangladesh, has officially signed a MOU with Brac Bank, led by MD Mahiul Islam, Deputy Managing Director & Head of Retail Banking, establishing the bank as the hotel’s Premium Ramadan Partner. This collaboration aims to elevat

item.title: BERC keeps LPG price unchanged for March
item.description: The country's energy regulator has kept the 'official' price of 12-kg liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) cylinder unchanged at Tk 1,341 each for the month of March, although its market price is 50 per cent higher at around Tk 2,000. The Bangladesh Energy Regulatory Commission (BERC) fixed the price as

item.title: Rainfall offers some respite to sweltering Dhaka
item.description: Dwellers of Dhaka city got much-needed relief from the scorching heat on Monday as rain pelted the capital, bringing down temperatures and easing the discomfort caused by the recent heatwave. The sudden showers, accompanied by gusty winds, cooled the streets and offered respite to residents who ha

item.title: DUCSU holds gayebana janaza for Khamenei
item.description: Leaders of Dhaka University Central Students' Union (DUCSU) have held a gayebana janaza (funeral prayer in absentia) for Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, following his death. The prayer took place at the VC Chattar on the Dhaka University campus around 10:40 pm on Sunday. DUCSU Vice

item.title: Dhaka University observes National Flag Hoisting Day
item.description: The Dhaka University (DU) observed the National Flag Hoisting Day on Monday, marking the historic occasion with due solemnity and respect. The programme began in the morning with the hoisting of the national flag of independent Bangladesh in front of the university's Arts Building. A commemorative

item.title: Demand slowdown pushes farm products to the ground, farmers in peril
item.description: A broad-based slowdown in demand has dragged down prices of key farm products---potatoes, onions and eggs---pushing thousands of farmers into losses for a second straight year. This raises concerns among economists about a possible ripple effect on employment, real wages and overall economic growt

item.title: US embassy in Riyadh hit by drones
item.description: The US embassy in Riyadh was hit ​by two drones resulting in a limited fire ‌and some material damage, the kingdom's defence ministry said in a post on X on Tuesday, citing an initial assessment. The ​drone attack came amid ongoing Iranian missile ​and drone strikes on Gulf states that host ⁠

item.title: Trump says US has ‘virtually unlimited supply’ of munitions
item.description: US President Donald Trump on Monday said America has a “virtually unlimited supply” of munitions to use as the war with Iran rages. Trump, writing on his Truth Social website, said: “The United States Munitions Stockpiles have, at the medium and upper medium grade, never been hig

item.title: Tk 19.52b project aims to unlock Sylhet’s trade, tourism potentials
item.description: Known for its scenic landscape but constrained by poor road infrastructure, Sylhet division is poised for a major overhaul as the government moves ahead with a Tk 19.52 billion (Tk 1,952 crore) project to upgrade and widen crucial upazila and union roads. The project, titled “Widening and St
